updates.title: Do you have to pay tax on your NRE deposit interest? Key rules NRIs should know
updates.summary: NRE accounts allow NRIs to deposit foreign income in Indian rupees, facilitating free repatriation. Interest on NRE fixed deposits is exempt from Indian income tax, enhancing their appeal.

updates.title: ITR filing AY 2026-27: How to claim TDS refund while filing your income tax return
updates.summary: Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) is a way through which tax is collected in advance by deducting a certain amount from specified payments. A TDS refund becomes applicable when the amount of tax deducted from your income during a financial year is more than the tax you are actually required to pay.

updates.title: CAGR vs XIRR: Which is better for analysing mutual fund returns?
updates.summary: The Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) is the average annual rate of return of a lump-sum investment over a specific period. The Extended Internal Rate of Return (XIRR) is a return calculation method used for investments with multiple cash flows occurring on different dates.
